reasoning: The bill passed its first reading in November 2025. In January 2026, there were reports of it being fast-tracked. However, as of mid-to-late February 2026, it was still in committee, and on February 14, it was reported that PM Netanyahu is pushing to soften the wording of the bill. This indicates potential legislative roadblocks and disagreements within the coalition. As of March 15, 2026, there is no news of the bill having passed its second and third readings. The deadline is April 1, 2026, leaving only about two weeks. The Knesset is primarily occupied with the budget, which must be passed by March 31. The combination of legislative friction and the extremely tight timeline to clear the final hurdles drastically lowers the probability of it officially becoming law before April 1, 2026.
